title: Thandi and the Magic School Shoes
synopsis: When Thandi finds a pair of sparkly shoes in her classroom, she discovers they have magical powers that make her dance uncontrollably. With help from her diverse group of friends, she learns to control the magic and uses it to help everyone have the best school day ever.

pages.text:
Thandi loved her new school. She had made lots of friends already. There was Carlos who loved to draw. Maya who knew all about bugs. And Jin who could whistle any song.

But today was different. Today, Thandi found something very special in the classroom corner.

illustration.description: A bright, colorful classroom with diverse children playing and learning. Thandi, a young Black girl with curly hair in colorful barrettes, stands near a reading corner with cushions and books. Carlos (Latino boy) is drawing, Maya (South Asian girl) is looking at a bug book, and Jin (East Asian boy) is smiling. Warm morning sunlight streams through windows.
illustration.alt: Thandi and her diverse friends in their cheerful classroom

pages.text:
"Look what I found!" Thandi called out to her friends.

In the dress-up bin were the most beautiful shoes she had ever seen. They were bright purple with silver sparkles. They seemed to shimmer and glow.

"Wow!" said Maya. "Those are pretty!"

"Can you try them on?" asked Carlos.

illustration.description: Thandi holding up magical purple shoes with silver sparkles that seem to glow with a soft light. Her friends Maya, Carlos, and Jin gather around her with excited expressions and big curious eyes. The dress-up corner has colorful costumes and accessories scattered around. The shoes have an ethereal, fairy-tale quality.
illustration.alt: Thandi showing her friends the magical sparkly shoes

pages.text:
Thandi slipped on the shoes. They fit perfectly!

"How do they feel?" asked Jin.

"They feel... tingly," said Thandi. "And warm. And... OH!"

Suddenly, Thandi's feet started to move all by themselves. She began to dance and spin around the classroom!

illustration.description: Thandi with surprised wide eyes as her feet begin to move on their own, sparkles swirling around the magical shoes. Her arms are outstretched for balance as she starts an involuntary dance. Her friends watch with amazement and concern. The classroom background shows movement lines and magical sparkles in the air.
illustration.alt: Thandi beginning to dance uncontrollably in the magic shoes

pages.text:
Thandi couldn't stop dancing! She twirled past the art table. She spun by the book corner. She leaped over the toy blocks.

"Help!" she giggled. "I can't stop!"

Her friends tried to catch her, but she was dancing too fast. Even Mrs. Johnson, their teacher, was surprised.

illustration.description: Thandi dancing wildly through the classroom with a mix of joy and panic on her face, magical sparkles trailing behind her. Mrs. Johnson (a kind Black woman with natural hair) watches with surprise. Maya, Carlos, and Jin try to help but can't catch up. Classroom items are slightly scattered from the magical dancing.
illustration.alt: Thandi dancing uncontrollably around the classroom while everyone tries to help

pages.text:
"The shoes are magic!" said Maya.

"We need to help Thandi!" said Carlos.

"But how?" asked Jin.

Mrs. Johnson had an idea. "In fairy tales, magic always has rules. We need to figure out the rules!"

Thandi danced by the window. She danced by the door. Then something interesting happened.

illustration.description: Mrs. Johnson pointing thoughtfully while the children brainstorm together. Thandi continues dancing in the background, but now they're all thinking hard about solutions. Thought bubbles with fairy tale elements (crowns, wands, castles) float above their heads. The scene has a problem-solving, teamwork atmosphere with warm classroom lighting.
illustration.alt: The teacher and students working together to solve the magic shoe problem

pages.text:
When Thandi danced near her friends, the shoes slowed down a little. When she danced alone, they went faster.

"The magic gets stronger when you're alone!" said Maya.

"And weaker when you're with friends!" added Jin.

"Everyone hold hands with Thandi!" said Carlos.

They all joined hands in a circle.

illustration.description: The children forming a circle holding hands with Thandi in the center, all with determined and hopeful expressions. The magical sparkles from the shoes are calming down, becoming softer and more controlled. Mrs. Johnson smiles approvingly in the background. The scene has a warm, cooperative feeling with golden light suggesting the magic is being tamed.
illustration.alt: Friends joining hands in a circle to help control the magic shoes

pages.text:
It worked! When they all held hands, Thandi could control the dancing. The shoes still wanted to dance, but now she could choose how.

"This is amazing!" said Thandi. "I can dance however I want, but only when we're all together!"

"Can we dance too?" asked Maya.

"Let's all dance!" said Thandi.

illustration.description: All the children dancing together in a joyful group, with Thandi leading them in the magical shoes. The sparkles now create beautiful patterns around all of them, not just Thandi. Their faces show pure happiness and friendship. Mrs. Johnson claps along. The classroom feels magical and celebratory with swirling, controlled sparkles.
illustration.alt: All the friends dancing together with magical sparkles around them

pages.text:
Soon, other kids wanted to join their dance. Emma came over. Then Aiden. Then Zara and Marcus.

The magic shoes seemed to love it when more friends joined in. The sparkles grew brighter and prettier. The whole classroom was filled with dancing and laughter.

"This is the most fun ever!" shouted Emma.

illustration.description: A diverse group of children all dancing together - Emma (a girl with red braids), Aiden (a boy with blond hair), Zara (a Middle Eastern girl with a hijab), and Marcus (a Black boy with glasses). The magical sparkles now encompass the entire classroom, creating rainbow patterns. Everyone has huge smiles and the scene radiates joy and inclusivity.
illustration.alt: Many diverse children dancing together in the sparkly, magical classroom

pages.text:
When it was time to put the shoes away, Thandi wasn't sad. She had learned something important.

"Magic is better when you share it," she said to her friends.

"And friends make everything more fun," said Carlos.

"Even magic shoes!" added Jin.

Thandi put the shoes back in the dress-up bin. But she knew they would always be there when the class wanted to dance together again.

illustration.description: Thandi carefully placing the now-gently glowing shoes back in the colorful dress-up bin, surrounded by her close friends. Their faces show contentment and friendship. The shoes emit a soft, peaceful sparkle, suggesting they're happy too. The classroom is tidy again, and warm afternoon light creates a cozy, satisfied atmosphere showing the end of a perfect day.
illustration.alt: Thandi putting the magic shoes away while surrounded by her happy friends

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Where did Thandi find the magic shoes?
questions.explanation: Thandi found the sparkly magic shoes in the dress-up bin in the classroom corner.

questions.question: What happened when Thandi put on the shoes?
questions.explanation: The magic shoes made Thandi dance and spin around the classroom without being able to stop.

questions.question: How did Thandi's friends help her control the magic?
choices: They held hands in a circle
questions.explanation: When all the friends joined hands in a circle with Thandi, she could control the dancing shoes.

questions.question: What did Thandi learn about magic?
choices: Magic is better when you share it
questions.explanation: Thandi learned that magic is better when you share it with friends.

questions.question: Who is Thandi's teacher?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Johnson is Thandi's teacher who helped figure out the rules of the magic shoes.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: Thandi found the shoes in the ___ bin.
The shoes were bright purple with silver ___.
Thandi couldn't stop ___ when she wore the shoes.

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instructions: Complete these sentences about Thandi's adventure.

sentences.text: The magic got ___ when Thandi was with her friends.
All the children held ___ in a circle to help Thandi.
Thandi learned that magic is better when you ___ it.
